response.justification: James is a versatile, experienced engineer with strong software craftsmanship, testing culture, and some high-throughput systems exposure, but he falls well short of the core minimum qualifications: his C++ experience is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project, versus the required 8 years, and his background centers on web/full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang rather than large-scale cloud compute infrastructure. He also lacks a formal technical-leadership track record mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers, making him a poor fit for this specific role despite solid general seniority.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and active mentoring via workshops and courses
Broad, adaptable polyglot background spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
concerns: C++ experience (~7 months professional) far below the required 8 years
No dedicated cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure ownership; experience is mostly product/full-stack
No formal technical-lead role managing priorities, deadlines, and cross-team alignment

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ experience vs. hard requirement
Strong testing, mentoring, and distributed-systems fundamentals
